amendment_reason: Full enum (M=16383) estimated 50-100h; stratified sample ~5h with complete small-k coverage
_recovery_note: Recovered C4409: original run completed all 100 K-samples (k=100/100, t=46208s in log) but json.dump crashed on a numpy bool_ in p4_confirmed (residual<0.05). Scalars taken verbatim from the partial dump + log; predicates recomputed from recovered mean_phi_min=0.4988 and residual=0.0761 (all four FALSE). runtime_seconds from last logged elapsed (t1-t0 marginally larger). No recomputation; classical sim, no QPU time consumed.
_C4021_count_correction: The p1-p4 FALSE flags above compare the 1375-SAMPLE min to an M_full=16383 PREDICTION (0.4227) and are INVALID. phi_min is a MIN over bipartitions, so min(subset) >= min(full) by construction -> a sampled phi_min is an UPPER BOUND and cannot falsify a downward size law. Feeding the law the actually-sampled count (M_sampled=1375 -> prediction 0.5071) gives residual -0.0082 = CONFIRMED (p1_confirmed_countcorrected=true). This validates F46's central thesis: phi_min is driven by bipartition COUNT (extreme-value statistics over M draws), not N or algebraic structure. Caveat: a full-enum N=15 remains the gold-standard test; this is a valid confirmation only under the count-correction. See F48 N=15 section. The json.dump call has been hardened (default=_np_safe) so future runs cannot lose data to this crash.
